title: Intent-Derived Continuation First Class
goal: Make unsatisfied larger intent visible as first-class continuation pressure even when no human-created external issue exists.
non_goals: Do not complete the full generated CLI migration in this slice.
Do not introduce host-specific tracker semantics into core planning.

intent.outcome: Finished-work inspection and compact summary surface derived follow-up candidates from archived closeouts whose larger intent remains open.
intent.constraints: Use checked-in planning/package surfaces first; keep the implementation provider-agnostic and machine-first.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest summary/report behavior that makes unsatisfied intent hard to lose.
intent.escalation: Stop before redesigning the full autopilot loop or adding a new workflow subsystem.

execution.milestone: Expose intent-derived continuation candidates from machine-first archived execplans.
execution.next_step: Archive this slice and promote the next autopilot-loop enforcement slice when selected.
execution.proof: packages/planning focused tests, full planning installer tests, planning-surface checks, summary output, and reconcile output.

invariants: External trackers remain optional evidence, not the source of package continuation truth.
Archived execplans remain proof/history, but unsatisfied intent derived from them must affect summary guidance.
No broad implementation should proceed from derived continuation without active planning promotion.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Autopilot and ordinary execution should plan, implement, evaluate, and continue until the user/product intent is satisfied or explicitly routed.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After this slice, promote the next bounded autopilot-loop enforcement slice if summary still only reports candidates rather than executing an evaluate/continue loop.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: The package can notice unsatisfied larger intent from its own archived closeout records and surface a concrete next owner.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Full autopilot loop enforcement, commit blocking for all unsatisfied intent, and generated-CLI migration implementation.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Finished-work inspection must inspect machine-first .plan.json archives, not only legacy Markdown archives.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: Run focused planning package tests and planning summary/reconcile checks.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: Harden autopilot so derived continuation candidates are promoted and re-evaluated in a loop.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Correct. It should be first-class behaviour. Autopilot should plan -> implement -> evaluate in a loop until intent is satisfied. Even without autopilot, the goal must not be allowed to be lost, and should be communicated to the user in some way.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Make intent-derived continuation a product behavior rather than a manual review habit.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Expose derived continuation candidates in finished-work inspection and execution readiness from archived closeouts with unsatisfied larger intent.
intent_interpretation.interpretation distance: medium; this is the first enforceable slice of the broader autopilot loop.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check that no external issue is required for the package to communicate unsatisfied intent.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: planning package summary/inspection implementation and tests; active planning state and this execplan
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "finished_work_inspection or intent_validation or execution_readiness"; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run agentic-workspace reconcile --format json
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Any redesign of archive format, external tracker ingestion, or full autopilot orchestration.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: generated CLI migration implementation, GitHub host-specific adapters, or broad unrelated roadmap lanes.

stop_conditions.stop when: Derived continuation candidates are visible in compact summary and narrow tests pass.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: The next change requires new autopilot orchestration rather than summary/report pressure.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Work expands into all generated CLI migration or all open issue ingestion.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: Planning summary cannot distinguish partial closeout from fully landed work.

context_budget.live working set: planning summary, finished-work inspection, execution readiness, focused package tests, active execplan
context_budget.recoverable later: historical archived plans, unrelated roadmap lanes, generated CLI migration implementation details
context_budget.externalize before shift: update execution_run, finished_run_review, intent_satisfaction, closure_check, and execution_summary
context_budget.pre-work config pull: uv run agentic-workspace start --format json; uv run agentic-workspace skills --target . --task ... --format json
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: not needed for this bounded planning package slice
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Continue from this active execplan and the focused tests around finished-work inspection.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: tool failure, package test failure, or need to redesign autopilot

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Make unsatisfied intent hard to lose.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Provider-agnostic, checked-in, machine-first, no broad redesign.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact field names and compact projection shape.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: Need to change product direction or make autopilot mutate planning automatically.

immediate_next_action: Archive this completed slice with the larger autopilot loop left open as required continuation.
invariants: Do not require a human-created issue before unsatisfied intent is visible.
Do not treat derived candidates as permission for broad work without promotion.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: derived_follow_up_candidates is advisory execution pressure derived from checked-in closeout evidence.
open_questions_to_close: Does compact summary communicate the candidate without opening raw archive files?
validation_commands: uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "finished_work_inspection or intent_validation or execution_readiness"
completion_criteria: Machine-first archived .plan.json files are inspected by finished-work inspection.
Partial or reopened closeouts emit derived follow-up candidates.
Compact summary includes the candidates and execution readiness prioritizes promotion before unrelated broad work.

execution_run.what happened: Finished-work inspection now reads machine-first archived .plan.json records, emits derived follow-up candidates for partial or reopened closeouts, and compact summary carries a bounded sample plus omitted count.
execution_run.scope touched: planning package summary/inspection implementation, focused planning tests, and planning state
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "finished_work_inspection or intent_validation or execution_readiness"; uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q; uv run ruff check packages/planning; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run agentic-workspace reconcile --format json
execution_run.result for continuation: The package now communicates unsatisfied intent without a human-created issue; full autopilot loop enforcement remains open.
execution_run.next step: Archive this slice and promote the autopilot-loop enforcement follow-on when ready.

finished_run_review.intent served: partially; the goal is no longer lost and is communicated by summary, but autopilot still needs loop enforcement.
finished_run_review.config compliance: used package startup, skills, summary, and planning surfaces before implementation.
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: medium; this is intentionally the first enforceable slice, not the full autopilot implementation.

proof_report.validation proof: uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q; uv run ruff check packages/planning; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run agentic-workspace reconcile --format json
proof_report.proof achieved now: finished-work inspection derives continuation candidates from machine-first archived execplans and compact summary exposes them.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: Focused tests cover .plan.json archived closeouts, derived candidates, and execution readiness when no active plan is present.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Correct. It should be first-class behaviour. Autopilot should plan -> implement -> evaluate in a loop until intent is satisfied. Even without autopilot, the goal must not be allowed to be lost, and should be communicated to the user in some way.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: finished-work inspection derives continuation candidates from machine-first archived closeouts and compact summary communicates them

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Reduce successful-completion cost across restart, handoff, review, proof, and follow-on work.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Prefer one queryable package surface that communicates required continuation over relying on chat or external issues.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can an agent recover unsatisfied larger intent without a human-created follow-up issue?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: the bounded communication/detection slice landed, but full plan-implement-evaluate autopilot enforcement remains open
closure_check.evidence carried forward: planning package tests, ruff, planning-surface check, summary, and reconcile passed
closure_check.reopen trigger: autopilot proceeds past unsatisfied intent without promotion or summary stops surfacing derived candidates

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Finished-work inspection now derives follow-up candidates from machine-first archived closeouts with unsatisfied or reopened larger intent, and compact summary communicates a bounded sample plus omitted count.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q; uv run ruff check packages/planning; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run agentic-workspace reconcile --format json
execution_summary.follow-on routed to: Derived finished-work continuation candidates and the next autopilot-loop enforcement slice.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: The durable product lesson is encoded in finished-work inspection behavior and this archived closeout; no memory note needed.
execution_summary.resume from: Promote the next bounded autopilot-loop enforcement slice if continuing this larger intent.

discard.summary: No Memory, docs, or config promotion was needed for local execution detail.

continuation.summary: Full autopilot plan -> implement -> evaluate loop enforcement remains a follow-on after derived candidates are visible.

drift_log: 2026-04-27: Initial plan created from user feedback that intent-derived continuation must be first-class behavior.
